sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check the initial state of Level 2 (history[9].frame)
level2_init = history[9].frame
seg = level2_init.segmentation
tiles = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 4:
        boundary = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in boundary)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in boundary)
        tiles[(min_r, min_c)] = n['color']

init_yellow = [pos for pos, color in tiles.items() if color == 'Y']
print(f"Level 2 initial yellow tiles: {init_yellow}")
print(f"Level 2 initial (26,35): {tiles.get((26,35), 'N/A')}")
print(f"Level 2 initial (35,35): {tiles.get((35,35), 'N/A')}")

# Current state
seg_cur = current_frame.segmentation
cur_tiles = {}
for n in seg_cur['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 4:
        boundary = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in boundary)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in boundary)
        cur_tiles[(min_r, min_c)] = n['color']

cur_yellow = [pos for pos, color in cur_tiles.items() if color == 'Y']
print(f"\nCurrent yellow tiles: {cur_yellow}")
print(f"Current (26,35): {cur_tiles.get((26,35), 'N/A')}")
print(f"Current (35,35): {cur_tiles.get((35,35), 'N/A')}")

# Check if current state matches initial state
if init_yellow == cur_yellow:
    print("\nCurrent state matches Level 2 initial state!")
else:
    print(f"\nCurrent state differs from initial: init={init_yellow}, cur={cur_yellow}")

# Let me try clicking the confirm button to see what happens
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 32, 'col': 4}])

# Check result
print(f"\nAfter confirm: step={current_frame.step}, level={current_frame.level}")
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
